---
title: "Week 4: String Operations Reference"
subtitle: "Complete Guide to String Slicing and Methods"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
  pdf:
    toc: true
---

# String Slicing Reference

## Basic Slicing Syntax

```python
string[start:end:step]
```

- **start**: Starting index (inclusive)
- **end**: Ending index (exclusive)
- **step**: Step size (default 1)

## Slicing Examples

### Basic Slicing

```python
text = "Python Programming"
#       012345678901234567  # Index positions

# Get substring
print(text[0:6])    # "Python"
print(text[7:18])   # "Programming"
print(text[0:4])    # "Pyth"

# From start/to end
print(text[:6])     # "Python" (from beginning)
print(text[7:])     # "Programming" (to end)
print(text[:])      # "Python Programming" (complete copy)
```

### Negative Indexing

```python
text = "Hello World"
#       01234567890  # Forward
#      -11        -1  # Backward

# Negative indices
print(text[-1])     # "d" (last character)
print(text[-5:])    # "World" (last 5 characters)
print(text[:-6])    # "Hello" (all but last 6)
print(text[-5:-1])  # "Worl" (remember: end is exclusive!)
```

### Step Parameter

```python
text = "ABCDEFGHIJ"

# Every other character
print(text[::2])    # "ACEGI"
print(text[1::2])   # "BDFHJ"

# Every third character
print(text[::3])    # "ADGJ"

# Reverse string
print(text[::-1])   # "JIHGFEDCBA"

# Reverse every other
print(text[::-2])   # "JHFDB"
```

### Common Patterns

```python
# Get file extension
filename = "document.pdf"
extension = filename[-3:]  # "pdf"
name = filename[:-4]       # "document"

# Extract parts
email = "user@domain.com"
at_index = email.find("@")
username = email[:at_index]      # "user"
domain = email[at_index+1:]      # "domain.com"

# Remove first/last character
text = "Hello"
without_first = text[1:]   # "ello"
without_last = text[:-1]   # "Hell"
without_both = text[1:-1]  # "ell"
```

---

# String Methods Reference

## Case Transformation

### `.upper()` - Convert to uppercase
```python
text = "Hello World"
print(text.upper())         # "HELLO WORLD"
print("python".upper())     # "PYTHON"
```

### `.lower()` - Convert to lowercase
```python
text = "Hello World"
print(text.lower())         # "hello world"
print("PYTHON".lower())     # "python"

# Common use: case-insensitive comparison
if user_input.lower() == "yes":
    print("User agreed")
```

### `.title()` - Title case
```python
text = "hello world from python"
print(text.title())         # "Hello World From Python"

# Note: handles apostrophes
print("it's great".title()) # "It'S Great" (not perfect!)
```

### `.capitalize()` - Capitalize first letter
```python
text = "hello world"
print(text.capitalize())    # "Hello world"
```

### `.swapcase()` - Swap case
```python
text = "Hello World"
print(text.swapcase())      # "hELLO wORLD"
```

## Whitespace Methods

### `.strip()` - Remove edge whitespace
```python
text = "  Hello World  \n\t"
print(text.strip())         # "Hello World"

# Strip specific characters
text = "***Hello***"
print(text.strip("*"))      # "Hello"
```

### `.lstrip()` - Remove left whitespace
```python
text = "  Hello World  "
print(text.lstrip())        # "Hello World  "
```

### `.rstrip()` - Remove right whitespace
```python
text = "  Hello World  \n"
print(text.rstrip())        # "  Hello World"
```

## Search Methods

### `in` operator - Check if substring exists
```python
text = "Hello World"
print("World" in text)      # True
print("world" in text)      # False (case sensitive!)
```

### `.find()` - Find substring position
```python
text = "Hello World World"
print(text.find("World"))   # 6 (first occurrence)
print(text.find("xyz"))     # -1 (not found)

# Find starting from position
print(text.find("World", 7)) # 12 (second occurrence)
```

### `.index()` - Like find but raises error
```python
text = "Hello World"
print(text.index("World"))  # 6
# print(text.index("xyz"))  # ValueError! (not found)
```

### `.count()` - Count occurrences
```python
text = "Mississippi"
print(text.count("i"))      # 4
print(text.count("ss"))     # 2
print(text.count("z"))      # 0
```

### `.startswith()` - Check start
```python
url = "https://example.com"
print(url.startswith("https"))  # True
print(url.startswith("http"))   # True
print(url.startswith("ftp"))    # False
```

### `.endswith()` - Check end
```python
filename = "document.pdf"
print(filename.endswith(".pdf"))  # True
print(filename.endswith(".doc"))  # False
```

## Transformation Methods

### `.replace()` - Replace substring
```python
text = "Hello World"
print(text.replace("World", "Python"))  # "Hello Python"

# Replace multiple occurrences
text = "apple apple apple"
print(text.replace("apple", "orange"))  # "orange orange orange"

# Limit replacements
print(text.replace("apple", "orange", 2))  # "orange orange apple"
```

### `.split()` - Split into list
```python
# Split by delimiter
data = "apple,banana,cherry"
print(data.split(","))      # ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']

# Split by whitespace (default)
text = "Hello   World   Python"
print(text.split())         # ['Hello', 'World', 'Python']

# Limit splits
text = "a-b-c-d"
print(text.split("-", 2))   # ['a', 'b', 'c-d']

# Split lines
text = "Line1\nLine2\nLine3"
print(text.split("\n"))     # ['Line1', 'Line2', 'Line3']
```

### `.join()` - Join list into string
```python
# Join with delimiter
words = ["Python", "is", "great"]
print(" ".join(words))      # "Python is great"
print("-".join(words))      # "Python-is-great"
print("".join(words))       # "Pythonisgreat"

# Join with newlines
lines = ["Line 1", "Line 2", "Line 3"]
print("\n".join(lines))     # Multi-line string
```

## Validation Methods

### `.isalpha()` - Check if all letters
```python
print("Hello".isalpha())    # True
print("Hello123".isalpha()) # False
print("Hello World".isalpha()) # False (space!)
```

### `.isdigit()` - Check if all digits
```python
print("123".isdigit())      # True
print("123.45".isdigit())   # False (decimal point)
print("123abc".isdigit())   # False
```

### `.isalnum()` - Check if alphanumeric
```python
print("Hello123".isalnum()) # True
print("Hello 123".isalnum()) # False (space!)
print("Hello_123".isalnum()) # False (underscore)
```

### `.islower()` - Check if lowercase
```python
print("hello".islower())    # True
print("Hello".islower())    # False
print("hello123".islower()) # True (ignores non-letters)
```

### `.isupper()` - Check if uppercase
```python
print("HELLO".isupper())    # True
print("Hello".isupper())    # False
print("HELLO123".isupper()) # True (ignores non-letters)
```

### `.isspace()` - Check if all whitespace
```python
print("   ".isspace())      # True
print(" \t\n".isspace())    # True
print(" a ".isspace())      # False
```

---

# Common String Patterns

## Email Validation
```python
email = "user@domain.com"

# Basic validation
if "@" in email and email.count("@") == 1:
    username, domain = email.split("@")
    if "." in domain and len(username) > 0:
        print("Valid email format")
```

## Password Strength
```python
password = "MyPass123"

# Check criteria
has_upper = any(c.isupper() for c in password)
has_lower = any(c.islower() for c in password)
has_digit = any(c.isdigit() for c in password)
long_enough = len(password) >= 8

if all([has_upper, has_lower, has_digit, long_enough]):
    print("Strong password")
```

## Input Cleaning
```python
# Clean user input
user_input = "  John DOE  "
clean_name = user_input.strip().title()

# Remove extra spaces
text = "Hello    World"
clean_text = " ".join(text.split())
```

## CSV Parsing
```python
# Parse CSV line
line = "John,25,Engineer"
name, age, job = line.split(",")
age = int(age)  # Convert to number
```

## URL Components
```python
url = "https://www.example.com/path/page"

# Extract parts
protocol = url[:url.find("://")]
without_protocol = url[url.find("://")+3:]
domain = without_protocol[:without_protocol.find("/")]
path = without_protocol[without_protocol.find("/"):]
```

---

# Performance Tips

## String Building

```python
# INEFFICIENT: String concatenation in loop
result = ""
for word in words:
    result = result + word + " "

# BETTER: Join list
result = " ".join(words)

# BETTER: Build list then join
parts = []
for item in items:
    parts.append(process(item))
result = "".join(parts)
```

## Method Chaining

```python
# Chain methods for efficiency
clean_email = email.strip().lower()

# But don't overdo it - readability matters!
# BAD: Too much chaining
result = text.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_").split("_")[0].upper()

# BETTER: Break into steps
clean = text.strip().lower()
underscored = clean.replace(" ", "_")
first_word = underscored.split("_")[0]
result = first_word.upper()
```

---

# Common Mistakes

## Forgetting Strings are Immutable
```python
# WRONG
text = "hello"
text[0] = "H"  # TypeError!

# RIGHT
text = "H" + text[1:]
```

## Not Reassigning After Methods
```python
# WRONG
name = "john"
name.upper()
print(name)  # Still "john"

# RIGHT
name = name.upper()
print(name)  # "JOHN"
```

## Off-by-One in Slicing
```python
# Getting 3 characters starting at index 1
text = "ABCDEF"
# WRONG: text[1:3] gives "BC" (only 2!)
# RIGHT: text[1:4] gives "BCD"
```

## Empty String Handling
```python
# WRONG
text = ""
first = text[0]  # IndexError!

# RIGHT
if text:
    first = text[0]
else:
    first = ""
```

Remember: Practice makes perfect! The more you work with strings, the more natural these operations become.
